text.title: SEO Core Rules for SPAs
text.summary: Concrete rules for SSR, meta tags, canonical URLs, and crawlable link structure in Single Page Applications.

rule.statement: Render critical page content in the initial HTML response — never rely on client-side JS for content that must be indexed.
rule.rationale: Search engine crawlers may not execute JavaScript, or may index the page before hydration completes. Content absent from the initial HTML is at risk of not being indexed.

rule.statement: Use SSR or SSG for all public-facing pages; defer CSR-only rendering to authenticated or non-indexed routes.
rule.rationale: Next.js getStaticProps/getStaticPaths and ISR (revalidate) provide SSG with on-demand regeneration — the best performance/freshness trade-off for most content sites.

example.note: Next.js page with ISR — content in initial HTML, revalidated every hour.

antipattern.description: Fetching product data in useEffect and rendering into DOM after hydration.
antipattern.reason: The initial HTML contains no product content — crawlers may index an empty shell.

rule.statement: Every public page must have a unique title (under 60 chars), meta description (under 160 chars), canonical URL, Open Graph tags, and Twitter Card tags.
rule.rationale: Missing or duplicate meta tags cause indexing conflicts; Open Graph tags control how the page appears when shared on social platforms.

rule.statement: Set the canonical URL to the definitive version of the page URL to prevent duplicate content penalties.
rule.rationale: Query parameters (e.g. ?ref=email, ?page=1) can create duplicate URL variants; canonical signals the preferred version to crawlers.

example.note: Next.js Head with full meta tag set for a product page.
example.code: <Head> <title>{product.name} | Example Store</title> <meta name="description" content={product.description.slice(0, 160)} /> <link rel="canonical" href={`https://example.com/products/${product.id}`} /> <meta property="og:type" content="product" /> <meta property="og:title" content={product.name} /> <meta property="og:description" content={product.description} /> <meta property="og:image" content={product.image} /> <meta property="og:url" content={`https://example.com/products/${product.id}`} /> <meta name="twitter:card" content="summary_large_image" /> </Head>

antipattern.description: A single static title and description shared across all pages of a SPA.
antipattern.reason: Every page gets the same snippet in search results; click-through rate drops and duplicate content is flagged.

rule.statement: All navigable links must use anchor tags with a valid href attribute — never onClick-only divs or buttons for navigation.
rule.rationale: Crawlers follow href links; onClick handlers that call router.push() are invisible to bots and create orphaned pages that receive no link equity.

example.note: No href — invisible to search engine crawlers.
